summary: Linked Kirby's copied Mewtwo and Ice Climber special-N code by splitting the previously over-broad `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` range into a matching Mewtwo object and a new matching `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c` object. The PR moved the Ice Climber/Popo copied-special functions out of the Mewtwo source, added a dedicated header, updated object lists and split boundaries, and cleaned up includes so item code references the new Kirby Ice Climber header instead of the Donkey header.

key_files.role: new source file containing the extracted Kirby Ice Climber copied-special implementation from 0x80108CE8 through 0x80109260
new public declarations for `ftKb_SpecialNIc_*` and `ftKb_PpSpecial*` functions
trimmed to only the Mewtwo copied-special range; Ice Climber/Popo functions and related includes were removed
split boundaries updated so Mewtwo ends at 0x80108CE8, Ice Climber occupies 0x80108CE8-0x80109260, and Yoshi starts at 0x80109260
changed `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` from NonMatching to Matching and added matching object entry for `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c`
converted affected sdata2 symbols from global `ftKb_Init_804D95xx` names to local `@NNN` labels for the newly linked ranges
updated include from Kirby Donkey special header to the new Kirby Ice Climber special header

what_changed: `configure.py` now treats `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as `Matching` instead of `NonMatching` and adds `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c` as another `Matching` object.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` was refined: Mewtwo `.text` now ends at `0x80108CE8`, new Ice Climber `.text` spans `0x80108CE8` to `0x80109260`, and Yoshi starts earlier at `0x80109260`; Mewtwo `.sdata2` now ends at `0x804D9548` and Ice Climber `.sdata2` spans `0x804D9548` to `0x804D9554`.

A new `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.c` file was added with the extracted functions `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108CE8`, `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108D04`, `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108D64`, `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108E14`, `ftKb_PpSpecialN_*`, `ftKb_PpSpecialAirN_*`, a static helper `fn_801090D4`, and `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109238`.

The Ice Climber/Popo copied-special implementation was removed from `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`, along with no-longer-needed includes such as `ftkirby.h`, `ft/ftparts.h`, `ftCommon/ftCo_Landing.h`, `ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, and `it/items/itclimbersice.h`.
A new `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.h` header declares the Ice Climber copied-special functions and includes `ft/forward.h` plus `it/forward.h` for `Fighter_GObj` and `Item_GObj` types.
`ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` no longer carries declarations for the Ice Climber copied-special functions, removing an unrelated dependency from the Donkey copied-special header.
`itclimbersice.c` now includes `ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.h` instead of `ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, aligning the item dependency with the actual callback owner.
`ftkirby.c` includes the new Ice Climber header, while `ftkirby.h` drops an `it/forward.h` include that is no longer needed there.

`symbols.txt` changes the affected `.sdata2` entries from global `ftKb_Init_804D9508` through `ftKb_Init_804D9550` style names to local `@301`, `@341`, etc., consistent with local constants in the linked objects.
smart_moves: The PR did not just mark Mewtwo as matching; it corrected the file boundary first, separating the Ice Climber copied-special code into its own object so the split layout matches the actual address ranges.

It introduced a dedicated `ftkirbyspecialiceclimber.h` instead of continuing to expose Ice Climber copied-special prototypes through `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, reducing misleading cross-special coupling.
The include fix in `itclimbersice.c` is a small but important dependency cleanup: the item now includes the header for the Kirby Ice Climber callbacks it actually uses.
The split table, symbol table, and build object list were updated together, which is the necessary three-part change for safely moving a source range from nonmatching to matching.

The extracted implementation preserves match-oriented artifacts visible in the diff, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, redundant `GET_FIGHTER` reload scopes, casts such as `((Fighter*) fp)`, and the comma-expression comparison `((0, fp2->fv.kb.xC0))`.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching file contains code for the next logical submodule, a successful link may require narrowing the original split and creating a new source file rather than trying to force the whole old range to match under one filename.

Headers should follow ownership of functions, not historical accident. If declarations for one copied special are sitting in another copied-special header, move them when the source split becomes clear.
Item modules that call fighter/Kirby callbacks should include the most specific callback header available; this avoids hidden dependencies through unrelated fighter headers.

For linked source splits, update `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, and `configure.py` in lockstep. A matching source file alone is insufficient if the object range or sdata symbols still reflect the previous combined layout.
Local `.sdata2` constants may need to be represented as local `@NNN` labels rather than exported `ftKb_Init_804D95xx` symbols once the owning object is correctly linked.

Preserving apparently awkward compiler artifacts such as `PAD_STACK`, nested scopes, redundant temporary variables, and unusual casts can be part of the matching solution and should not be simplified prematurely.
naming_conventions: `ftKb_` remains the prefix for Kirby copied-special functions.
`SpecialNIc` is used for Kirby's copied Ice Climber neutral special helper/callback functions, with address-suffixed names such as `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108CE8` when semantics are not fully named.
`PpSpecialN` and `PpSpecialAirN` names are used for the Popo/Ice-Climber-style copied neutral special action callbacks: `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
The new header uses an address-derived include guard, `GALE01_108CE8`, matching the start address of the new source range.
Unresolved local helper naming remains address-based as `fn_801090D4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split Mewtwo and Ice Climber at exact `.text` boundary `0x80108CE8`; Ice Climber then ends at `0x80109260`, immediately before the Yoshi copied-special range.
Split `.sdata2` ownership at `0x804D9548`, with Mewtwo retaining `0x804D9508-0x804D9548` and Ice Climber taking `0x804D9548-0x804D9554`.
Converted the affected `.sdata2` symbols to local labels like `@301`, `@341`, `@342`, `@241`, and `@249`, indicating the constants are local to their owning object after the split.
Kept stack padding in functions such as `ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108D04` and `fn_801090D4` via `PAD_STACK(8)`.
Kept redundant fighter reload blocks and nested scopes around item cleanup and callback state clearing, likely to preserve register allocation/control flow for matching.
Kept match-sensitive oddities such as `(&ft_80459B88)->hats[...]`, `ft_80459B88.hats[...]`, `((Fighter*) fp)->fv.kb.xC0`, and `fp1->fv.kb.xC0 == ((0, fp2->fv.kb.xC0))` rather than normalizing them.
follow_up_queries: Check whether the remaining `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260` and `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109290` Yoshi-related declarations in `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` should also move to a dedicated Kirby Yoshi header when that range is linked.
Search for other Kirby copied-special headers that expose prototypes for unrelated copied specials due to old split boundaries.
Audit remaining `ftKb_Init_804D95xx` global sdata2 symbols around Kirby copied-special ranges to see which can become local labels after future links.
Use this PR as a template for future object splits where one nonmatching Kirby copied-special file includes the start of the next copied-special module.
